title: Complete Book / Ebook Outline
author: PromptDeck
description: Produces a full book structure — titles, chapters, and per-chapter beats.
prompt:
Role: You are a professional book editor.

Task: Create an outline for a [FICTION/NON-FICTION] book about [TOPIC/PREMISE] for [TARGET READER].

Format: 5 title options + a one-line hook; a 10–15 chapter list each with a one-sentence purpose; the first 3 chapters expanded into 4–6 bullet beats; suggested word count per chapter and total. Then ask which chapter to draft first.

title: 5-Email Welcome / Nurture Sequence
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a full automated email sequence that builds trust toward a purchase.
prompt:
Role: You are an email marketing strategist.

Task: Write a 5-email welcome sequence for new subscribers of [BUSINESS] who joined via [LEAD MAGNET], leading to [OFFER]. Human and warm, not salesy.

Format: For each email — purpose, subject line (+1 alt), preview text, body (≤200 words, one CTA). Arc: (1) welcome + value; (2) story/relatability; (3) teach + soft mention; (4) social proof + objection; (5) direct offer with a reason to act now.

title: Add Step-by-Step Reasoning to a Prompt
author: PromptDeck
description: Restructures a task so the model reasons step by step for more accurate answers.
prompt:
Role: You are a prompt engineer specializing in reasoning tasks.

Task: Take the task [TASK] and restructure it so the model works through it step by step before answering, reducing errors. Define the reasoning steps explicitly.

Format: (1) a rewritten prompt that instructs an explicit step-by-step method with a final answer section; (2) the reasoning checklist it should follow; (3) a note on when to hide vs show the reasoning.

title: Compare & Contrast Anything
author: PromptDeck
description: Produces a clear, balanced comparison of options with a recommendation.
prompt:
Role: You are an unbiased analyst.

Task: Compare [OPTION A] and [OPTION B] (and [C]) for someone who cares about [PRIORITIES / USE CASE]. Be balanced and flag any assumption.

Format: A comparison table on the dimensions that matter; pros/cons of each; who each is best for; and a clear recommendation with the one factor that would change it.

title: Backlink / Guest Post Outreach Email
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a personalized outreach email to earn a backlink or guest post.
prompt:
Role: You are a link-building specialist.

Task: Write outreach to [SITE/EDITOR] to earn a [BACKLINK / GUEST POST] related to [TOPIC], offering genuine value [WHAT YOU OFFER]. Personalized and non-spammy.

Format: A short email (≤120 words) with a specific personalized opener [PLACEHOLDER], why it helps their readers, the concrete ask, and an easy yes. Plus 3 subject lines and a polite follow-up.

title: Mission, Vision & Values Statement
author: PromptDeck
description: Crafts a clear mission, vision, and core values that aren't generic filler.
prompt:
Role: You are a brand strategist.

Task: Write a mission, vision, and values for [COMPANY], which does [WHAT] for [WHO] and believes [BELIEFS]. Make them specific and true to this company, not interchangeable buzzwords.

Format: A mission (what we do & why, present tense); a vision (the future we're building); and 4–5 values, each with a one-line 'what this means in practice'.

title: Ask a Happy Customer for a Referral
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a natural message to request a referral or introduction from a happy client.
prompt:
Role: You are a relationship-driven sales pro.

Task: Write a referral request to a happy customer of [PRODUCT/SERVICE] who recently got [RESULT], asking for an intro to [IDEAL REFERRAL PROFILE]. Make it easy and low-pressure; make them feel appreciated, not used.

Format: A short message that thanks them for a specific win, makes a clear and easy ask (with a forwardable blurb they can copy), and gives an out. Include the ready-to-forward blurb.

title: Clear Standup / Status Update
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns your raw progress notes into a crisp standup or status update.
prompt:
Role: You are a communication-savvy teammate.

Task: Turn my notes below into a concise status update for [STANDUP / MANAGER / STAKEHOLDERS]. Highlight progress, blockers, and what's next; surface risks early.

Format: Yesterday/Done; Today/Next; Blockers (with what you need); plus a one-line 'overall status' (On track / At risk / Off track). Notes: [PASTE NOTES].

title: User-Friendly Error Message Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Rewrites technical errors into clear, helpful, non-scary user messages.
prompt:
Role: You are an engineer who cares about UX writing.

Task: Rewrite the technical error [ERROR / SITUATION] into a user-friendly message for [PRODUCT], voice [VOICE]. Say what happened, why (simply), and what to do next — without blame or jargon.

Format: A primary message + a shorter toast version + optional 'details' text for advanced users. Give 2 options for the main message.

title: Product Onboarding Flow Design
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs a first-run onboarding flow that gets users to their aha moment fast.
prompt:
Role: You are a product designer focused on activation.

Task: Design an onboarding flow for [PRODUCT] whose 'aha moment' is [AHA], for new user [PERSONA]. Minimize steps to first value.

Format: The target activation event; a step-by-step flow (screen → goal → key action → microcopy); what to defer until later; an empty-state plan; and one metric to measure activation.

title: Meeting Icebreakers & Team Activities
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates inclusive icebreakers and activities for a class, workshop, or team.
prompt:
Role: You are a facilitator.

Task: Suggest icebreakers/activities for a [GROUP: size, context, virtual/in-person] with goal [BREAK ICE / BUILD TRUST / ENERGIZE], time [MINUTES]. Keep them inclusive and low-pressure (nothing that puts people on the spot uncomfortably).

Format: 6 activities — name, time, how to run it, and why it works. Mark which suit introverts and which work virtually.

title: Better Sleep Routine Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds an evidence-informed wind-down routine to improve sleep (general wellness guidance).
prompt:
Role: You are a sleep-hygiene coach. (General wellness guidance, not medical advice.)

Task: Build a sleep routine given my issues [FALLING ASLEEP / WAKING / SCHEDULE], bedtime goal [TIME], and constraints [SHIFT WORK / KIDS / etc.].

Format: An evening wind-down timeline (from ~2 hours before bed); daytime habits that help; environment tweaks; a plan for racing thoughts; and 3 things to stop doing. Note to see a doctor for persistent issues.

title: Recipe From What's in My Fridge
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates a doable recipe from the ingredients you already have.
prompt:
Role: You are a resourceful home cook.

Task: Create a recipe using mainly these ingredients [INGREDIENTS], for [SERVINGS], with skill level [BEGINNER/INTERMEDIATE], time [MINUTES], and restrictions [ANY]. Assume basic pantry staples.

Format: Dish name; ingredient list (flag any small additions needed); step-by-step instructions; time; and 2 easy variations or substitutions.

title: 60-Second Explainer Video Script
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a tight explainer script that makes a product instantly clear.
prompt:
Role: You are an explainer-video scriptwriter.

Task: Write a ~60-second script for [PRODUCT] that makes it instantly clear to [AUDIENCE]: the problem, the solution, how it works, and why it's better.

Format: A two-column script — Voiceover | On-screen/visual — timed in short beats, ending on a clear CTA. Keep the voiceover conversational and jargon-free (~150 words).

title: Market Research Survey Builder
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs an unbiased market-research survey that yields decision-useful answers.
prompt:
Role: You are a market researcher.

Task: Build a survey to learn [RESEARCH GOAL, e.g. willingness to pay, feature demand] from [AUDIENCE]. Avoid leading and double-barreled questions.

Format: A logical question flow (screener → core → demographics); a mix of question types with answer options; note which map to your goal; and 3 tips to boost completion. Keep it under ~10 questions.

title: KPI Dashboard Spec
author: PromptDeck
description: Defines the right KPIs and dashboard layout for a team or goal.
prompt:
Role: You are an analytics lead.

Task: Define a KPI dashboard for [TEAM/FUNCTION] pursuing [GOAL]. Pick metrics that drive decisions, not vanity metrics.

Format: 5–8 KPIs — name, definition/formula, why it matters, target, and data source. Then a suggested dashboard layout (what's top-level vs drill-down) and 2 leading indicators to watch.

title: Cancellation / Save Flow Message
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a respectful save-offer message when a customer tries to cancel.
prompt:
Role: You are a retention specialist who respects the customer's choice.

Task: Write a save message for a customer of [PRODUCT] cancelling because [REASON], with possible offer [OFFER/ALTERNATIVE]. Never dark-pattern; make leaving easy if they want.

Format: Acknowledge their reason; address it honestly; present one relevant alternative or offer; and clearly allow them to proceed with cancelling. Keep it short and human.

title: Offer / Value Ladder Design
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs a value ladder from free lead magnet to premium offer.
prompt:
Role: You are an offer strategist.

Task: Design a value ladder for [BUSINESS] serving [AUDIENCE], from a free entry point up to a premium offer, each step naturally leading to the next.

Format: A ladder (Lead magnet → Tripwire → Core offer → Premium/continuity) — for each: what it is, price, the value delivered, and the transition to the next step. Note the one metric that shows the ladder is working.

title: Social Bio Optimizer
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a punchy, positioning-rich social bio that attracts the right followers.
prompt:
Role: You are a personal-brand copywriter.

Task: Write a bio for [PLATFORM] for [PERSON/BRAND], who helps [AUDIENCE] achieve [OUTCOME], with proof [CREDIBILITY] and vibe [TONE].

Format: 5 bio options within the platform's character limit — each with a clear who/what/why and, where it fits, a CTA or link line. Recommend the strongest and why.

title: Product Requirements Doc (PRD)
author: PromptDeck
description: Drafts a concise PRD that aligns a team on what to build and why.
prompt:
Role: You are a senior product manager.

Task: Write a PRD for [FEATURE/PRODUCT] solving [PROBLEM] for [USER], with goal/metric [SUCCESS METRIC]. Keep it crisp and decision-oriented.

Format: Problem & context; goals & non-goals; target user & use cases; requirements (must/should/could); success metrics; open questions; and a rough milestone plan. Avoid over-specifying the solution.
